stories.headline: Aurora man arrested in fatal shooting over stolen bicycle dispute
stories.summary:
A 40-year-old Aurora man, Lewis Anthony Davis Jr., was arrested Wednesday on suspicion of first-degree murder after fatally shooting an acquaintance at an apartment complex in the 14100 block of East Colorado Drive early Tuesday morning. The victim, Joseph Giovanni Gorrocino, 48, was found outside with multiple gunshot wounds and was pronounced dead at the scene, with the Arapahoe County Coroner's Office ruling the death a homicide.

According to an arrest affidavit, the two men had spent the day together at the apartment pool when a dispute erupted over a bicycle Gorrocino had borrowed without permission and which was subsequently stolen. Surveillance video obtained by detectives showed Davis walk out of the apartment ahead of Gorrocino and then turn and shoot him multiple times as he exited.

Davis, who also faces a charge of possession of a weapon by a previous offender, was taken into custody by Aurora Police Department's SWAT team and fugitive and narcotics unit near Billings Street and Colfax Avenue. He is being held without bail. Davis previously pleaded guilty to vehicular homicide and assault in Arapahoe County in 2005. The 18th Judicial District Attorney's Office will file formal charges.

stories.headline: Aurora police watchdog urges task force after three fatal shootings of people in mental health crises
stories.summary:
The independent monitor overseeing court-ordered reforms to the Aurora Police Department has called on city officials to conduct a broad review of three recent fatal police shootings in which officers killed individuals experiencing mental health crises. In a special 12-page report released Wednesday, monitor Jeff Schlanger requested an investigation into the systemic circumstances surrounding each shooting, including victims' mental health histories, prior warning signs and interactions with public safety systems.

The report was triggered primarily by the April 9 shooting of 23-year-old Amare Garlington, who was suicidal and charged at officers with a butcher's knife at an Aurora apartment complex before being shot by Officer Mark Moore. The report also cites the September 2024 shooting of 17-year-old Blaze Balle-Mason, who called 911 claiming to be armed before running at officers, and the May 2025 killing of 32-year-old Rashaud Johnson, who was unarmed and walking toward an officer after a physical altercation near Denver International Airport.

Schlanger is calling for Aurora to form a task force with representatives from police, fire, city government and mental health service providers to identify gaps in the city's behavioral health safety net. He emphasized the need to break down institutional silos and examine what can be done differently to prevent lethal outcomes in crisis situations. The city had not publicly responded to the report's recommendations as of Thursday.

stories.headline: Colorado governor candidates spar over business climate and housing at Glendale forum
stories.summary:
Four of the five major-party candidates running for Colorado governor gathered Thursday at a forum in Glendale to address the state's business competitiveness and housing affordability. Democratic candidates U.S. Sen. Michael Bennet and Attorney General Phil Weiser were joined by Republican state Sen. Barbara Kirkmeyer and nonprofit leader Victor Marx at the event hosted by 1st Bank/PNC and Land Title Guarantee, which drew several hundred business leaders.

Weiser cited the experience of a Durango-based aerospace company that found it faster and easier to expand into Oklahoma than to obtain permits in Colorado, arguing the state must adopt a more solutions-oriented approach. Kirkmeyer, pointing to her 20 years as a county commissioner and six years on the state's Joint Budget Committee, said eight years of one-party Democratic control had created an unaffordable state and pledged to make Colorado business-friendly from day one.

Bennet highlighted his private-sector background, including work for Phil Anschutz and as Denver school superintendent, as preparation for tackling the state's economic challenges. Marx, the only non-politician among the candidates, acknowledged a lack of policy expertise but said he would surround himself with knowledgeable advisors. Colorado's June 30 primary is roughly a month away.

stories.headline: Colorado primary election guide: What voters need to know ahead of June 30 ballot
stories.summary:
Colorado's 2026 primary election is scheduled for Tuesday, June 30, with competitive races on the ballot ranging from the U.S. Senate to county commissioner contests. County clerks may begin mailing ballots to registered active voters as early as June 8, and Colorado's same-day registration policy means voters can register up until election day itself.

The U.S. Senate Democratic primary features incumbent John Hickenlooper being challenged by progressive state Sen. Julie Gonzales of Denver. Numerous other statewide races are also contested, including attorney general, secretary of state and the open governor's race, with both major parties fielding multiple candidates. The Libertarian Party and Unity Party of Colorado also have contested primary races.

Colorado Sun and CPR News have both published comprehensive voter guides to help residents navigate the crowded field of candidates. Results from county clerks will begin to be released at 7 p.m. on June 30.

stories.headline: Polis signs abortion medication access, wildlife crossing fee and other bills into law
stories.summary:
Gov. Jared Polis signed roughly two dozen bills into law Wednesday and Thursday, including a measure requiring Colorado colleges and universities with student health centers to provide access to abortion medication starting Aug. 1, 2027. Under House Bill 1335, campuses with pharmacies must stock the two-pill prescription regimen; others can have medical staff write prescriptions or directly dispense the medication. The bill passed with only Democratic support.

Polis also signed Senate Bill 141, which creates an optional $5 collision prevention fee that Coloradans can add to their vehicle registration starting in 2027, with proceeds funding wildlife crossing infrastructure. The Colorado Department of Transportation reports that wildlife crossing projects have reduced wildlife-involved crashes by approximately 90% in most locations, and estimates suggest as many as 14,100 such crashes occur in Colorado annually.

Among other bills signed, House Bill 1065 allows local governments to apply for new financing for public transit infrastructure and creates an income tax credit of up to $8.33 million annually for housing investment in transit corridors. House Bill 1110 requires banks to report suspected financial exploitation of adults aged 70 or older to police and allows institutions to delay fund disbursement for up to 180 days.

stories.headline: Former Colorado Avalanche Stanley Cup champion Claude Lemieux dies at 60
stories.summary:
Claude Lemieux, a former Colorado Avalanche player and four-time Stanley Cup champion, died at the age of 60, the NHL Alumni Association announced Thursday. Lemieux took his own life, according to authorities.

Lemieux was a key figure in the Avalanche's Stanley Cup championship runs and is widely remembered for his physical, combative style of play during his NHL career. He played for multiple teams over his career but is particularly associated with the Avalanche and the New Jersey Devils, with whom he won championships.

Lemieux had been honored by the Avalanche organization at a game against the New Jersey Devils in Denver in recognition of his contributions to the franchise. His death comes during an already difficult offseason for the Avalanche organization following their first-round playoff sweep.

stories.headline: Denver Summit FC delays Centennial Stadium opening until July 18 due to weather-related construction setbacks
stories.summary:
The Denver Summit, the city's National Women's Soccer League franchise, announced Thursday that the opening of its temporary Centennial Stadium will be delayed from its originally planned date to July 18, citing weather-related construction delays. Controlling owner Rob Cohen attributed the setback to recent rainfall that interrupted road and sidewalk pouring during a critical phase of construction.

The club's July 3 home match against the Kansas City Current and the July 12 match against the Houston Dash will instead be held at Dick's Sporting Goods Park, where the team has played its most recent home games. The additional capacity at Dick's Sporting Goods Park will allow more tickets to be released for both fixtures, with new availability going on sale June 4.

The Centennial Stadium, designed to seat 12,000 fans, broke ground in June 2025 and is intended to serve as the club's home through the 2027 season. The Summit's permanent home at Santa Fe Yards is slated to open in 2028.

stories.headline: Jokić named All-NBA First Team, Murray earns Third Team honors for Nuggets
stories.summary:
Denver Nuggets center Nikola Jokić has been named to the All-NBA First Team, continuing his streak as one of the league's most decorated players. Teammate Jamal Murray earned All-NBA Third Team recognition, marking another strong individual season for the Nuggets' backcourt leader.

The honors come as the Nuggets look ahead to the offseason following their postseason run. Jokić's selection reinforces his standing as one of the premier players in the NBA, while Murray's recognition reflects his continued development as a franchise cornerstone alongside Jokić.

The dual All-NBA selections give Denver one of the most decorated player pairings in the Western Conference heading into the 2026-27 season.

stories.headline: Denver nonprofit breaks ground on $58.5 million mixed-income housing project in Uptown
stories.summary:
A Denver nonprofit broke ground Thursday on a $58.5 million housing development in the Uptown neighborhood, roughly 18 months after acquiring the parking lot site where the project will rise. The development, known as Pearl Street Uptown Apartments, includes a new nine-story building and the renovation of an existing property, with completion expected by late 2027.

The project was structured using creative financing that avoids the use of low-income housing tax credits, an approach that organizers say allowed for greater flexibility in designing the mixed-income development. Jeff Kraft, deputy director of the Colorado Office of Economic Development and International Trade, spoke at the groundbreaking ceremony.

The development adds to a growing pipeline of housing projects in Denver's Uptown corridor as the city continues to grapple with affordability pressures. The project's completion is expected to bring additional affordable and market-rate units to a neighborhood with strong transit access.

stories.headline: Founding documents exhibition opens at History Colorado Center through June 14
stories.summary:
An exhibition featuring some of the most significant documents in American history opened Thursday at the History Colorado Center in Denver as part of a national tour timed to the country's 250th anniversary. Colorado is one of only eight states to host the "Freedom Plane National Tour: Documents That Forged a Nation," which runs through June 14 at 1200 Broadway.

The collection includes an 1823 original engraving of the Declaration of Independence commissioned by John Quincy Adams, a rare draft copy of the U.S. Constitution with handwritten delegate notes from the 1787 Constitutional Convention, the Treaty of Paris of 1783, the Articles of Association of 1774 and the oaths of allegiance signed by George Washington, Alexander Hamilton and Aaron Burr. Organizers said the documents have never before traveled together outside Washington, D.C.

Admission to the exhibition is free, with general admission to the History Colorado Center available for $20. Children and members of History Colorado enter at no cost. The tour is backed by the National Archives and Records Administration and the National Archives Foundation.

stories.headline: Outside Days festival expands to three days at Auraria Campus this weekend with major music lineup
stories.summary:
Colorado's premier outdoor adventure festival returns this weekend under a new name — Outside Days — at a new location, the Auraria Campus in Denver, running Friday through Sunday. The event has grown from its 2024 debut as a two-day festival at Civic Center Park, with last year's edition drawing approximately 35,000 attendees.

The festival features a mix of music performances, outdoor adventure films and speakers. Friday's headliner is Death Cab for Cutie, Saturday's lineup includes My Morning Jacket and The Flaming Lips, and Sunday closes with Cage the Elephant along with local bands N3ptune and The Mañanas. Noted climber Alex Honnold will appear for a live podcast recording, and National Geographic photographer Keith Ladzinski and Radiohead's Ed O'Brien are among the scheduled speakers.

The film lineup, curated with Mountainfilm, includes 11 titles covering topics from Olympic cross-country skier Jessie Diggins' battle with an eating disorder to the history of the Schwinn Bicycle Company. Tickets start at $115 and are available online.

stories.headline: Severe afternoon and evening storms expected across Denver metro Thursday, unsettled weather to continue
stories.summary:
Severe thunderstorms were forecast to develop along the Front Range Urban Corridor Thursday afternoon and evening, with the primary threats being large hail and damaging winds. The storms were expected to move out by approximately 10 p.m., leaving mostly cloudy skies overnight with lows in the low to mid 50s.

Another round of storms is forecast for Friday afternoon beginning around 3 p.m. and continuing through the dinner hour, with a marginal severe weather risk covering the far northeastern corner of the state. An isolated tornado or two cannot be ruled out in that area alongside large hail and damaging winds.

The weekend is expected to be drier with only isolated storm chances Saturday and Sunday and seasonal daytime highs in the upper 70s to low 80s. Forecasters are tracking a potential summer-like heat wave for the Denver metro late next week, with highs possibly reaching the upper 80s during the first full week of June.

weather.notable: About 16 degrees above the average high for this time of year!

joke.joke: I wish I had the confidence of the person who puts 'Sent from my iPhone' at the bottom of every email, as if we're all impressed.

weekendEvents.description: The third annual outdoor-lifestyle festival takes over the Auraria Campus with three days of live music, films, speakers, and gear. Performers include Death Cab for Cutie, My Morning Jacket, Cage the Elephant, Tash Sultana, The Flaming Lips, and Goth Babe.

weekendEvents.description: Colorado's largest pop culture convention returns to the Colorado Convention Center with celebrity meet-and-greets, autograph sessions, cosplay, comics, anime, and gaming. Guests include stars from The X-Files, The Lord of the Rings, Scream, The Mandalorian, and more.

topStory.headline: Russian Drone Strikes Romanian Apartment Building, Injuring Two in First Casualty Incident on NATO Soil
topStory.summary:
A Russian Geran-2 drone, also known as a Shahed 136, struck a residential apartment building in the eastern Romanian city of Galati on Friday, detonating its full explosive payload and igniting a fire on the 10th floor. Two residents sustained abrasions and were transported to the Galati County Emergency Clinical Hospital, while approximately 70 people were evacuated from the building as emergency crews extinguished the blaze. Romanian officials said the drone's entire warhead detonated on impact.

Romanian President Nicusor Dan convened an emergency session of the country's Supreme Defence Council, calling the strike "the most serious incident to have affected Romanian territory since the start of Russia's war of aggression against Ukraine." Romania's foreign ministry summoned the Russian ambassador and formally requested that NATO accelerate the transfer of anti-drone capabilities to the country. France announced it would similarly summon the Russian ambassador in solidarity.

NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte spoke directly with President Dan following the incident, reaffirming that the alliance "stands ready to defend every inch of Allied territory" and pledging to enhance readiness against drone threats. European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen stated that Russia's "war of aggression has crossed yet another line." Russia had not issued a comment on the incident as of the time of reporting.

Romanian defence officials said two F-16 fighter jets were scrambled after the drone was detected, but Brigadier General Gheorghe Maxim noted that forces had only four minutes between detection and impact, leaving little time to respond. Maxim also acknowledged a significant operational constraint: Romanian forces cannot fire munitions into Ukrainian airspace, as Ukraine remains an active war zone. Russian drones have strayed across Romania's border on multiple previous occasions during the nearly four-year war, but this marks the first time Romanian citizens have been injured in such an incident, raising the stakes for NATO's eastern flank posture.
